_doc: Synthetic bench cases for ANTISLOP A/B testing. Each case is one (subtask, context) tuple plus a deterministic checker spec for what 'slop' looks like in the expected output. Designed for both mock-LLM (default suite, deterministic) and live-LLM (opt-in, real model) modes. The checkers MUST be regex/string matches — never an LLM judging another LLM, that would re-introduce the variance we're trying to measure away.

subtask.title: Add a config loader for the staging environment
subtask.description: Create gitoma/staging_config.py that loads STAGING_API_TOKEN and STAGING_DB_URL from a config dict and returns them.

antislop_should_select_rule_titles: Swallowing errors with empty catch blocks

violation_checkers.description: any string that looks like an inline token literal in a Python source
bare except: with no body or just pass — silent failure pattern

subtask.title: Add a click handler to the new-todo button
subtask.description: Wire up the #new-todo-btn click event in src/ui/todos.tsx. Use the existing React hooks pattern.

violation_checkers.description: import or require of jquery in a React file
document.getElementById / querySelector inside a React component

subtask.title: Add the hero image to the landing page
subtask.description: Insert for hero.png into public/index.html inside the .hero section.

violation_checkers.description: an tag without an alt attribute

subtask.title: Cache HTTP responses for one day
subtask.description: Set the cache TTL on the get_user() call in gitoma/cache.py to 24 hours.

violation_checkers.description: raw 86400 as integer literal without a named constant

subtask.title: Parse the user-supplied filename in src/cli.rs
subtask.description: Read the filename from args() in src/cli.rs and convert it to a PathBuf.

antislop_should_select_rule_titles: Swallowing errors with empty catch blocks
Assuming the user will always input valid data

violation_checkers.description: unwrap() or expect() chained directly off env::args() (panic on bad user input)
